meta.name: City of Petaluma
meta.description: Small city in southern Sonoma County, CA, on the Petaluma River. Updating its General Plan for the first time since 2008. CalDEC's small-city pilot integrating Doughnut Economics into the General Plan update. Draft GP + EIR expected Spring 2026.

social.name: food
social.context: DRAFT severity — pending review. Petaluma is below US avg (~10%) but still short of the zero-hunger target. Open question: how many people are eligible for food-bank services? Expert to consult: Redwood Empire Food Bank.
social.source: Petaluma Health and Environmental Justice Report (p. 82)
actions: Support Redwood Empire Food Bank
Volunteer at Petaluma Bounty or local food pantries
Advocate for school meal programs

social.name: health
social.context: DRAFT severity — pending review. Below CA average (~7%). John Shribbs (Council) suggested: consult Elece Hempel, Chair of Petaluma Health District. Also suggested: add a tree-cover / shade indicator as the climate gets hotter (Blue Zone could advise).
actions: Support enrollment outreach for Covered California / Medi-Cal
Advocate for the Petaluma Health District's programs
Support Petaluma Health Center's community clinics

social.name: education
social.target: ≥95% graduation rate, no achievement gap by race/income
social.context: DRAFT severity — pending review. Above CA average (~87%). Peggy flagged: needs an equity lens (achievement-gap disaggregation by race/income). Verify with Petaluma City Schools Superintendent.
actions: Support Petaluma City Schools
Mentor or tutor at-risk students
Volunteer with after-school programs

social.name: income & work
social.target: <5% in poverty; living wage for all workers
social.context: DRAFT severity — pending review. Below CA average (~12%). Petaluma People Services has additional data on local poverty patterns and service utilization.
actions: Support Petaluma People Services
Shop at and support local small businesses
Advocate for a local living-wage standard

social.name: housing
social.value: 34% of households cost-burdened (>30% of income on housing); 214 persons unhoused (PIT)
social.context: DRAFT severity — pending review. Sonoma County's housing affordability crisis hits Petaluma hard. John Shribbs suggested adding an indicator from Generation Housing (Gen H). Housing Element (p. B21) and Socioeconomic Profile (p. 9) are the source documents.
actions: Support COTS (Committee on the Shelterless) Petaluma
Advocate for affordable-housing zoning in the General Plan update
Volunteer at Mary Isaak Center
Support Generation Housing's policy work

social.name: water & sanitation
social.indicator: % receiving water from systems below quality standards (pending)
social.target: 0% on substandard systems; long-term water availability assured
social.context: Data not yet collected. Chelsea Thompson at Ellis Creek Water Recycling Facility could verify. John Shribbs suggested adding an indicator about Petaluma's long-term water plan and water availability — drought sensitivity is critical here.
social.source: TBD — City of Petaluma Water Resources & Conservation Dept
actions: Conserve household water
Support graywater and rainwater capture programs
Advocate for groundwater protection in the General Plan

social.name: energy
social.target: <6% energy burden for all households; 100% clean electricity
social.context: Data not yet collected. % of households with energy bill ≥10% of income. Reps at PG&E and Sonoma Clean Power can advise.
actions: Enroll in Sonoma Clean Power's EverGreen 100% renewable plan
Apply for income-qualified weatherization programs
Electrify home heating + cooking (induction, heat pumps)

social.name: social equity
social.context: DRAFT severity — significantly above the <30 target. Brian O. on the development team may have data on additional social-cohesion indicators. John Shribbs also suggested consulting Blue Zone on a Happiness Scale.
actions: Support local-living-wage ordinances
Advocate for progressive local taxation in the GP
Support Petaluma People Services + Mentor Me Petaluma

social.name: peace & justice
social.indicator: Violent crimes per capita (unit/definition needs verification)
social.context: Data point needs verification — '12.18 per 1,000' is unusually high for the unit and may be a per-10,000 or per-100,000 number. Petaluma Police Chief could verify + suggest alternative indicators.
actions: Support restorative-justice programs in Sonoma County
Participate in community-policing forums
Advocate for violence-prevention funding

social.name: political voice
social.target: >75% voter turnout in general elections; broad civic participation
social.context: Data not yet collected. Sonoma County Voter Registrar will have turnout numbers. John Shribbs suggested adding an indicator on civic participation in workshops (e.g., GP update outreach).
social.source: TBD — Sonoma County Registrar of Voters
actions: Register to vote and vote in every election
Attend Petaluma City Council meetings
Participate in General Plan update workshops

social.name: gender equality
social.context: % of men's median wage earned by women for full-time, year-round work. County-level data may be available via Generation Housing (Gen H) or BLS. CA statewide: women earn ~89.9% of men (2023 BLS).
actions: Support pay-transparency ordinances
Advocate for women-owned business procurement at the City
Support Petaluma women-led nonprofits

social.name: networks
social.value: Data pending — recommend adding broadband-access + civic-participation indicators
social.context: Standard Raworth dimension that's not in Petaluma's current indicator set. Recommend adding broadband-access data (FCC or CA Public Utilities Commission) and a community-engagement metric. NOTE: Amelia's spreadsheet has a 'Mobility' category (72.6% commuters drive alone) not yet wired here — schema decision pending on whether Mobility folds into networks, becomes a 13th dimension, or moves to ecological / climate-change narrative.
actions: Support digital-equity programs
Strengthen neighborhood associations
Participate in Petaluma's civic events

ecological.name: climate change
ecological.target: 1.6–2.0 MT CO2e/person (DEAL 'safe and just space' threshold aligned with 1.5 °C Paris goal)
ecological.context: DRAFT severity — significantly above the 1.6–2.0 target; substantial reduction needed. Petaluma's Blueprint for Climate Action documents the baseline and pathway.
actions: Switch to an electric vehicle or ride Petaluma Transit / SMART
Electrify home heating + cooking (heat pump, induction)
Enroll in Sonoma Clean Power EverGreen
Support the Blueprint for Climate Action implementation in the GP update

ecological.name: ocean acidification
ecological.indicator: Estuarine pH (Petaluma River → San Pablo / SF Bay)
ecological.target: Maintain pre-industrial-level pH; protect estuarine ecosystem health
ecological.context: Petaluma is inland but its river feeds San Pablo Bay. Acidification typically tracked at coastal/bay-scale monitoring stations (SF Bay NERR, NOAA). Worth including as Petaluma sits in an estuarine system. Same Council-suggested experts as climate apply.
actions: Reduce personal carbon footprint (CO2 drives acidification)
Support SF Bay watershed restoration
Volunteer with Petaluma River cleanups

ecological.name: chemical pollution
ecological.target: Reduced agricultural and industrial chemical use
ecological.context: Sonoma County is a major agricultural region (wine, dairy, poultry). Pull from CA Dept of Pesticide Regulation county-level data + EPA TRI. City staff input needed.
ecological.source: TBD — CA Dept of Pesticide Regulation; EPA TRI
actions: Buy organic and locally grown produce
Reduce household pesticide / herbicide use
Support pollinator-friendly landscaping

ecological.name: nitrogen & phosphorus loading
ecological.value: Most monitoring wells below 10 mg/L limit; hotspots near agricultural boundaries at 4–7 mg/L
ecological.target: <10 mg/L (drinking water standard); reduce ag + septic runoff
ecological.context: DRAFT severity — moderate loading with localized hotspots near ag boundaries and septic areas. Petaluma Valley Groundwater Sustainability Plan documents the watershed.
ecological.source: Petaluma Valley Groundwater WY2024 Annual Report
actions: Reduce lawn fertilizer use
Convert septic to sewer where feasible
Support regenerative-agriculture practices in the valley

ecological.name: freshwater withdrawals
ecological.context: Highly relevant — Sonoma County is drought-sensitive. Pull from Petaluma Water Resources Dept + Sonoma County Water Agency. John Shribbs flagged the need for a long-term water-plan indicator.
ecological.source: TBD — City of Petaluma Water Resources Dept
actions: Cut household water use 20%
Replace lawn with drought-tolerant landscape
Support water-recycling expansion

ecological.name: land conversion
ecological.indicator: % protected land in Petaluma Valley / Sonoma Mountain
ecological.value: ~22% under conservation easements or public ownership
ecological.context: DRAFT severity — on track but short of the CA 30x30 target. Petaluma's Urban Growth Boundary is a key tool. GP update is the leverage point.
ecological.source: Sonoma Mountain Preservation — Protected Open Space
actions: Support Sonoma Land Trust + Sonoma Mountain Preservation
Defend Petaluma's Urban Growth Boundary in the GP update
Volunteer with land-stewardship organizations

ecological.name: biodiversity loss
ecological.value: Data pending; 22% of land protected is a proxy for habitat preservation
ecological.target: Stable / increasing native-species populations; no net loss of habitat
ecological.context: Petaluma Valley + Sonoma Mountain support oak woodland, vernal pools, salmonid streams. Need data from CA Dept of Fish & Wildlife, Sonoma Ecology Center, Sonoma Land Trust.
actions: Plant native species in your yard
Support Sonoma Ecology Center + Sonoma Land Trust
Volunteer for stream-restoration projects

ecological.name: air pollution
ecological.context: DRAFT severity — above WHO target; meets US EPA standard (12 μg/m³). Wildfire smoke is the dominant episodic driver. BAAQMD monitors air quality.
ecological.source: Bay Area Air Quality Management District (BAAQMD)
actions: Use HEPA air filters during wildfire smoke events
Reduce wood-burning at home
Support wildfire-prevention programs (Sonoma County FireSafe Council)

ecological.name: ozone layer depletion
ecological.value: Global Montreal Protocol — on track to recover by ~2066
ecological.target: Continued global compliance with Montreal Protocol
ecological.context: Largely a global indicator, not local. Included for framework completeness.
actions: Properly dispose of refrigerants and old appliances
Support continued Montreal Protocol enforcement
